   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Returning To Los Angeles From Spain Before Start Of Playoffs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285093/Luka-Doncic-Returning-To-Los-Angeles-From-Spain-Before-Start-Of-Playoffs</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic is returning to Los Angeles on Friday after spending the past week in Spain receiving multiple treatments on his left hamstring strain. Doncic is rehabilitating a Grade 2 left hamstring strain.</p>
<p>The Los Angeles Lakers haven't provided a timetable for when Doncic may be able to return from his injury. The Lakers open their first round <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/odds/">playoff series</a> on Saturday against the Houston Rockets.</p>
<p>Grade 2 hamstring strains typically require a month recovery. Doncic missed part of the first round of the playoffs in 2022 with the Dallas Mavericks with a hamstring injury. Doncic eventually returned and helped lead the Mavericks to the Western Conference Finals.</p>
<p>The Lakers are also without Austin Reaves, who is dealing with an oblique injury.</p>
<p>Doncic is also petitioning the NBA to grant him regular season award eligibility after appearing in 64 games, one short of the minimum. Doncic missed two games in December for the birth of his daughter in Slovenia. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 16:50:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285093/Luka-Doncic-Returning-To-Los-Angeles-From-Spain-Before-Start-Of-Playoffs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Azzi Fudd Selected No. 1 In WNBA Draft By Dallas Wings</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285095/Azzi-Fudd-Selected-No-1-In-WNBA-Draft-By-Dallas-Wings</link>
      <description><p>Former UConn star Azzi Fudd was selected first overall by the Dallas Wings in the 2026 WNBA Draft, earning a $500,000 salary under the league's newly ratified collective bargaining agreement, nearly seven times what teammate Paige Bueckers earned as the top pick in 2025.</p>
<p>"I don't think it's fully sunk in. It's nothing I could have imagined," Fudd said. "Such a surreal feeling."</p>
<p>Fudd joins Bueckers in Dallas, reuniting the former Huskies teammates and giving UConn a record seven No. 1 overall selections in WNBA Draft history. Bueckers was present at the draft alongside several of Fudd's former Connecticut teammates.</p>
<p>"Paige is an incredible player, everyone knows that," Fudd said. "She's someone that makes playing basketball with easy."</p>
<p>Fudd averaged career highs across multiple categories in 2025-26, posting 17.3 points, 48 percent shooting, 45 percent from three, and 3.1 assists per game. She made 117 three-pointers this past season, fourth most in UConn history, and 196 over the past two seasons, the most in Division I over that span. Her path to Dallas included significant injury hurdles, including a torn ACL that limited her to just two games in 2023-24.</p>
<p>UCLA made history as the draft's deepest program. Lauren Betts went fourth to Washington, Gabriela Jaquez fifth to Chicago, and Kiki Rice sixth to expansion franchise Toronto, breaking UConn's record of four first-round selections set in 2002. The Bruins tied the all-time record of five total players drafted, shared previously by Tennessee, Notre Dame, and South Carolina.</p>
<p>Minnesota took TCU guard Olivia Miles with the No. 2 pick. Seattle selected Spain center Awa Fam Thiam third before Portland, in its first-ever pick as an expansion franchise, chose Spanish guard Iyana Martin Carrion.</p>
<p>Under the new CBA, second and third-round picks will earn $270,000, a figure that surpasses the previous maximum salary under the old agreement.</p>
<p>"I'm just blessed and grateful to come at this time," said No. 8 pick Flau'jae Johnson, who was traded to Seattle after being selected by Golden State. "My goal is to leave it better than I found it."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 21:03:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285095/Azzi-Fudd-Selected-No-1-In-WNBA-Draft-By-Dallas-Wings</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Fudd_Azzi_uconn_260404.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Davis Wants Championship Plan From Wizards Before Committing Long-Term</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285096/Anthony-Davis-Wants-Championship-Plan-From-Wizards-Before-Committing-Long-Term</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Davis made clear Monday that the Washington Wizards must present a credible path to contention this summer, setting up what figures to be one of the NBA offseason's most consequential front office conversations. Davis said he plans to meet with Monumental Basketball president Michael Winger and general manager Will Dawkins in the coming months to evaluate the franchise's competitive timeline.</p>
<p>Davis stopped well short of requesting a trade, acknowledging his contractual obligations while leaving the door open depending on what those discussions produce.</p>
<p>"Whether we figure something out in the summer (that's) good or bad, whatever ... if it doesn't get figured out, then come October, mid-October, late October, I will have a Washington Wizards uniform on, and I will go out there, and I will do everything I can to win a basketball game," Davis said. "In the summer, right now, I'm going to compete, train and have the mindset that I will be here next year. Not only because I'm under contract, but also, I like these young guys. I ain't going to lie. I feel like they're all my children."</p>
<p>Davis was direct about the difficulty of the Wizards' situation, expressing doubt that Washington can leap from the league's worst record in 2025-26 to a championship contender in one offseason, even with himself and Trae Young on the roster.</p>
<p>"I've been in this league a long time, and I've been with losing teams, been on losing teams," Davis said. "It's very hard to be a losing team and then a championship contender, right? I think one team has only done it: Boston in '08. Even when I was in New Orleans and we had two losing seasons, then we made the playoffs our third year - that, within itself, is a huge jump. What is our plan for winning going into next season? And how? Not like this is a plan, but how do we execute that? And based on that, we'll kind of see what happens."</p>
<p>Davis' contract adds another layer of complexity. He is owed $58.5 million in 2026-27 and holds a $62.8 million player option for 2027-28. Industry speculation suggests the 33-year-old may seek additional years on his current deal, though whether Washington would entertain that given his injury history and the organization's focus on its young core remains unclear.</p>
<p>Monday marked Davis' first formal availability with Washington-area media since his surprise trade from the Dallas Mavericks in early February. Despite a rocky initial perception of the franchise, Davis said his concerns faded quickly after arriving.</p>
<p>"I can personally say now it's not what people think or what people make it seem," Davis said. "Yes, the losing is part of it, so people kind of tie that with the organization. But the organization within itself is totally different from what people think they're seeing."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 22:59:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285096/Anthony-Davis-Wants-Championship-Plan-From-Wizards-Before-Committing-Long-Term</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Davis_Anthony_dal_260109.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James, Brandon Ingram Named NBA Players Of Week 25</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285092/LeBron-James-Brandon-Ingram-Named-NBA-Players-Of-Week-25</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James and Brandon Ingram have been named the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/player-of-the-week/30">NBA's Players of the Week</a> for Week 25.</p>
<p>James averaged 24.0 points, 6.0 rebounds and 9.7 assists as the Los Angeles Lakers went 3-1. James stepped into a bigger role with Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves out with injury. James and Michael Jordan are the only players to win the award while in their forties. Jordan won the award twice in the final weeks of his playing career while with the Washington Wizards. </p>
<p>Ingram averaged 25.5 points and 6.5 rebounds as the Toronto Raptors went 3-1 and secured the No. 5 seed in the Eastern Conference.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 16:37:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285092/LeBron-James-Brandon-Ingram-Named-NBA-Players-Of-Week-25</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_260212.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Pre-Playoff Betting Favorite Has Won 70 Percent Of NBA Championships Since 1980</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285089/Pre-Playoff-Betting-Favorite-Has-Won-70-Percent-Of-NBA-Championships-Since-1980</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder open the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">2026 NBA playoffs as championship favorites at +110</a>, carrying a 48 percent implied probability. Dating back to 1980, the pre-playoff betting favorite wins the championship roughly 70 percent of the time, though recent seasons have shown that edge is shrinking in the NBA's new parity era where no defending champion has even reached the Conference Finals since 2019.</p>
<p>The San Antonio Spurs (+500) and Boston Celtics (+550) trail the Thunder in the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/odds/">current title market</a>.</p>
<p>Between the Celtics in 2024 and the Thunder last season, the betting favorite has claimed the last two titles after a turbulent stretch from 2021 to 2023 when three straight favorites failed to reach the Finals.</p>
<p>From 2019 through 2025, pre-playoff favorites won just three of seven titles. That stands in contrast to the dynasty-driven 1980s and 1990s, when heavy favorites routinely delivered.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.covers.com/sportsoddshistory/nba-champs/">The most extreme favorites in recorded history according to Sports Odds History</a>, the 1996 and 1997 Chicago Bulls at -600 and -500 respectively, both won championships. The 2019 Golden State Warriors, also at -220, represent the starkest recent collapse, falling in the Finals despite carrying the highest implied probability of any modern contender. The Warriors played the first four games without Kevin Durant and fell down 3-1 to the Toronto Raptors. Durant tore his Achilles in Game 5, which was won by the Warriors, before the Raptors won Game 6 to win the series.</p>
<p>The Warriors' -220 line in 2019 has given way to a tighter +110 to +250 range in recent years, signaling that oddsmakers view championship races as considerably more open than during the league's dynasty eras.</p>

   <item>
      <title>Top-5 Net Rating Teams Each Also Had Top-10 Offensive, Defensive Efficiency</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285094/Top-5-Net-Rating-Teams-Each-Also-Had-Top-10-Offensive-Defensive-Efficiency</link>
      <description><p>Five NBA teams finished the 2025-26 regular season ranked in the top 10 in both offensive and defensive efficiency, a threshold that historically identifies legitimate championship contenders. Since 1974, roughly 75 percent of all NBA champions posted top-10 marks in both categories during the regular season before going on to win the NBA championship.</p>
<p>The Oklahoma City Thunder led the group on the defensive end, ranking first in defensive rating at 107.2, while posting a 119.2 offensive rating, good for seventh in the league. The San Antonio Spurs finished third defensively at 110.9 and fifth offensively at 119.3. The Detroit Pistons ranked second in defensive efficiency at 109.7 paired with a 10th-place offensive rating of 117.7.</p>
<p>The Boston Celtics posted the second-best offensive rating in the league at 120.7 and ranked fourth defensively at 112.8. The New York Knicks rounded out the group, finishing third offensively at 120.0 and eighth defensively at 113.4.</p>
<p>All five clubs also finished in the top five in <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/team-stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Team_Totals/Regular_Season/ediff/desc">overall net rating</a>, underlining the two-way consistency of the NBA's leading teams this season.</p>
<p>The Denver Nuggets and Cleveland Cavaliers have better <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">NBA championship odds entering the playoffs</a> than the Knicks and Pistons despite the statistical profile.</p>
<p>Those falling outside the top 10 in one category have typically compensated with a top-3 ranking in the other to remain viable title contenders. Teams outside both thresholds have rarely, if ever, won a championship in the modern era. The Houston Rockets in 1995 are the only team that finished the regular season outside the top-10 in both categories as a true historical outlier. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 21:01:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285094/Top-5-Net-Rating-Teams-Each-Also-Had-Top-10-Offensive-Defensive-Efficiency</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mazzulla_Joe_bos_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Immanuel Quickley Day-To-Day With Mild Hamstring Strain After MRI</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285091/Immanuel-Quickley-Day-To-Day-With-Mild-Hamstring-Strain-After-MRI</link>
      <description><p>The Toronto Raptors announced Monday that medical imaging has diagnosed guard Immanuel Quickley with a mild hamstring strain. Quickley is considered day-to-day.</p>
<p>Quickley averaged 16.4 points, a career-high 5.9 assists, 4.0 rebounds and 31.9 minutes in 70 games (all starts) this season. He scored 20 or more points in 20 games and recorded eight double-doubles. </p>
<p>Toronto will face the Cleveland Cavaliers in the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs. </p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Giannis Antetokounmpo Uncertain About Bucks Future, Won't Rule Out Extension</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285086/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Uncertain-About-Bucks-Future-Wont-Rule-Out-Extension</link>
      <description><p>Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered conflicting signals about his future with the Milwaukee Bucks following a season-ending 126-106 loss to the Philadelphia 76ers on Sunday. Antetokounmpo declined to confirm whether he has played his final game with the only franchise he has played for across his 13-year NBA career.</p>
<p>"That's a very good question," Antetokounmpo said when asked whether he has played his last game in a Milwaukee uniform. "I don't know. It's not up to me. We'll see."</p>
<p>The Bucks finished 32-50, snapping a nine-year postseason streak. Antetokounmpo was direct about where the franchise stands.</p>
<p>"We're very bad. We are the furthest away that we've been in the last couple of years," he said. "How many wins we have? 32? This is the second-lowest wins I've ever won. So yeah, we're the furthest away that we've been, I think. So, we'll see. This is what we have in front of us."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo expressed frustration about being held out of game action despite believing himself healthy after suffering a left knee hyperextension and bone bruise last month. He also pushed back on <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284900/Bucks-Tell-NBA-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Declined-Participating-In-3-On-3-Scrimmages-During-Return-Process">reports suggesting he refused to participate in practice</a>.</p>
<p>"At this point? Zero," Antetokounmpo said when asked if he had control over his situation. "I don't have control. Being cleared to play, I don't understand ... from my understanding, coming back to play, which I don't think I ever had any return-to-play protocol, but to my understanding was I had to play 3-on-3 to be able to be available to play. I did that multiple times. I've never in my life denied participation of practice. I don't know who said that, who came up with that, but that's disrespectful towards what I've done for this team and the way I carry myself my whole career, pretty much."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo also pointed to a broader breakdown in communication with the organization.</p>
<p>"I feel like sometimes people just don't listen," he said. "They listen to the sources. The main source is me. It is what it is. So again, do not disturb on my phone, go about my day, improve, and come back better."</p>
<p>On communication specifically, Antetokounmpo was pointed.</p>
<p>"I've always been open, but I don't know if that ... it's got to go both ways. It has to go both ways. That cannot go one way."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284567/Bucks-Ownership-On-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Hell-Be-Extended-Or-Hell-Be-Traded">Bucks owner Wes Edens told ESPN last month that Antetokounmpo will either be extended or traded before he can reach unrestricted free agency next summer.</a> The Bucks also listened to trade offers before February's deadline. When asked about a potential four-year, $275 million extension if he remains with Milwaukee through October, Antetokounmpo left the door open. </p>
<p>"We'll see when we get there," he said. "We have [several] months [until then] ... it's a long time. But somebody has to offer you that, for you to sign. I haven't been offered an extension. So, if that is on the table, then I will try to make the best decision for me and my family. But if it's not on the table, then I have to focus on how can I improve my worth and get on the floor and do what I do."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo closed with a measured assessment of his standing with the franchise.</p>
<p>"So, if everything goes well, hopefully, the Bucks want me here, why not? But if they don't? OK," said Antetokounmpo.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285074/Doc-Rivers-Bucks-Parting-Ways">Milwaukee will also conduct a head coaching search after Doc Rivers' departure was confirmed Sunday</a>. The franchise faces a pivotal offseason with its cornerstone player's future unresolved.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 07:13:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285086/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Uncertain-About-Bucks-Future-Wont-Rule-Out-Extension</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_251210.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>

      <description><p>The Brooklyn Nets have signed forward Trevon Scott to a contract.</p>
<p>Scott, 6-foot-8 and 225 pounds, originally joined Brooklyn on a 10-day hardship contract April 2. Scott averaged 8.0 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.4 steals in 31.2 minutes across five appearances.</p>
<p>The 26-year-old posted back-to-back career-high scoring efforts of 11 points against Indiana on April 9 and Milwaukee on April 10. He also set a personal best with four steals against the Bucks on April 7.</p>
<p>Scott spent the bulk of this season with Brooklyn's G League affiliate, the Long Island Nets, appearing in 47 total games and averaging 12.0 points, 4.7 rebounds, 2.0 assists, and 1.1 steals in 27.9 minutes per outing.</p>
<p>Across 199 career G League games with six affiliates, Scott has averaged 10.7 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.1 steals. His stops include the Salt Lake City Stars, Cleveland Charge, Agua Caliente Clippers, Greensboro Swarm, and Osceola Magic before landing with Long Island.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Patrick Dumont Optimistic About Mavs' Future With 'Generational Talent' Cooper Flagg</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285078/Patrick-Dumont-Optimistic-About-Mavs-Future-With-Generational-Talent-Cooper-Flagg</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Mavericks governor Patrick Dumont expressed confidence Sunday that 19-year-old Cooper Flagg gives the franchise a foundation to compete for championships, even as the franchise closes the season firmly in the lottery and faces critical decisions on basketball leadership and a new arena.</p>
<p>"Having a generational talent like Cooper Flagg should give everyone confidence, our fans, our team, that we're going to get back to the playoffs and that we're going to try to compete for championships as soon as we can," Dumont told the Dallas Morning News.</p>
<p>Dumont announced the franchise will begin an aggressive search for a new president of basketball operations Monday, targeting a mid-May hire. The position has been vacant since Nico Harrison's dismissal on November 11 following a 3-8 season start. Acting co-general managers Matt Riccardi and Michael Finley have managed basketball operations since and are considered internal candidates.</p>
<p>"Monday morning, we're going to get active, and we're going to talk to a lot of people," Dumont said. "Our goal is to have someone on board by the middle of May."</p>
<p>Dumont outlined his priorities for the role, stressing culture and communication alongside basketball acumen.</p>
<p>"They're going to set the tone for everything that we do from a basketball perspective," he said. "It's got to be a culture that will lead us to win championships and someone that can build that culture."</p>
<p>Sports Illustrated reported Dumont is targeting high-profile candidates including Oklahoma City Thunder vice president Sam Presti and Boston Celtics president Brad Stevens. Dumont declined to confirm names, citing league tampering rules.</p>
<p>"I can't talk to any of those people now because it would be tampering. So anything that you may have heard is just rumor and chatter," he said. "We'll figure out who's a great leader. Who has a track record. Who knows how to work with young players and develop them."</p>
<p>The presidential search runs parallel to a July 1 deadline to select a site for a new basketball-specific arena and 50-acre entertainment district. Dallas has narrowed options to the former Valley View Mall tract in North Dallas and a downtown location. Dumont cited Golden State's $1.4 billion Chase Center as the benchmark.</p>
<p>"I think Chase Center is definitely the leading benchmark, just because of the experience it provides to fans," he said. "The in-game experience, the viewing angles from seats. The amenities that it has."</p>
<p>On the February 2025 Luka Doncic trade and the franchise's difficult year, Dumont accepted responsibility.</p>
<p>"When expectations aren't met, we really have to take a critical look at everything we're doing," he said. "I really believe this is an extremely important offseason for this franchise. We're going to work tirelessly to get things right."</p>
<p>Minority owner Mark Cuban said in a late-March podcast that he regrets selling to the Adelson-Dumont families. Dumont responded diplomatically.</p>
<p>"Mark is really passionate about the Mavericks, and I think that's great," Dumont said. "I think he's really, really frustrated about the year that the team has had. And I understand that frustration.</p>
<p>"I consider Mark a friend. And look, I always appreciate his thoughts. And I appreciate his frustration.  </p>
<p>"But one thing I do want to say, in terms of ownership, I think we've been really clear: We're not moving the team to another city. The team is not for sale. We're going to continue to invest in this franchise. We're going to build a new home for the team, and hopefully in the long term we'll be able to celebrate that success for many years."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 22:00:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285078/Patrick-Dumont-Optimistic-About-Mavs-Future-With-Generational-Talent-Cooper-Flagg</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dumont_Patrick_dal_250210.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Billy Donovan Plans Meeting With Bulls' Ownership To Discuss Future</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285075/Billy-Donovan-Plans-Meeting-With-Bulls-Ownership-To-Discuss-Future</link>
      <description><p>Billy Donovan expressed uncertainty Sunday about his future with the Chicago Bulls, stating the organization has reached a "pivot point" following the dismissal of its top two basketball operations executives last week.</p>
<p>Donovan said he plans to meet with team president and CEO Michael Reinsdorf in the coming days to determine the path forward.</p>
<p>"I love being in Chicago and the organization and everything about it," Donovan said before the team's regular-season finale at the Dallas Mavericks. "But we're at this pivot point right now where everything has changed. Find out where we're all at on this. And how we're going to move forward."</p>
<p>The Bulls last week dismissed executive vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley. At a subsequent news conference, Reinsdorf voiced support for Donovan's continued role as head coach.</p>
<p>Reinsdorf previously stated that any front office candidate uninterested in retaining Donovan would likely not be the right fit. He later clarified in writing, however, that Donovan's status would not take "precedence in the process."</p>
<p>Donovan said outside opportunities would not factor into his decision, noting he had declined to pursue other openings in recent years out of loyalty to Chicago.</p>
<p>"I'm committed here to Chicago," Donovan said. "I'm going to try to be where my feet are planted. I'm going to stay there and stay committed to where I'm at."</p>
<p>Donovan holds a 226-255 record across six seasons in Chicago. He had been considered for the vacant head coaching position at the University of North Carolina before the school hired Michael Malone.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 12 Apr 2026 21:22:49 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Donovan_Billy_chi_240410.jpg" />

      <description><p>Doc Rivers is departing the Milwaukee Bucks as head coach. Rivers is stepping down after a 32-50 season. Milwaukee will be looking for their third head coach in the last three years.</p>
<p>In two-plus seasons on the Bucks sidelines, Rivers went 97-103. That's the Hall of Famer's only losing record over the five franchises he's coached.</p>
<p>Rivers led Milwaukee to the playoffs twice, but the Bucks exited in the first round each time. This season, the team didn't even advance to the Play-In Tournament.</p>
<p>The Bucks will reportedly pay out the final season on Rivers' deal in 2026-27. A new head coach will join the franchise in a critical juncture, as Giannis Antetokounmpo's future in Milwaukee remains in doubt.</p></description>
